heading: Hostile fire pay

children.text: subject to hostile fire or explosion of hostile mines;

on duty in an area in which the employee was in imminent danger of being exposed to hostile fire or explosion of hostile mines and in which, during the period on duty in that area, other employees were subject to hostile fire or explosion of hostile mines; or
killed, injured, or wounded by hostile fire, explosion of a hostile mine, or any other hostile action.

children.label: b
children.text: An employee covered by subsection (a)(3) who is hospitalized for the treatment of his or her injury or wound may be paid hostile fire pay under this section for not more than three additional months during which the employee is so hospitalized.

children.label: c
children.text: An employee may be paid hostile fire pay under this section in addition to other pay and allowances to which entitled, except that an employee may not be paid hostile fire pay under this section for periods of time during which the employee receives payment under because of exposure to political violence or payment under .section 5925 of this titlesection 5928 of this title
